other_observers: none; I was not the original finder of this bird. It had previously been reported on cobirds.
description: I observed the bird in the water, next to a Common Loon and several Western Grebes.  It had a similar build to the Western Grebes, but its neck was shorter (their heads were farther above the water with the neck extended).  Compared to the Common Loon, it was much paler, somewhat shorter, with a very much lighter build (lighter body, much thinner neck).  Its back was a medium brown, with light spots.  The back of its neck was a pale gray. The front half of the neck was whitish, with a gradual transition to the darker nape.  The face was whitish from about the eye on down, with a gradual transition to a light brownish cap.  The bill was narrow and pale, with an obvious upturned appearance.Behaviors: It was swimming in the water and occasionally diving, like a loon or grebe.Call: none heardPlumage: nonbreeding
